top_signals.title: Britain’s Electric Vehicle Market Is Booming as Fuel Prices Climb
top_signals.summary: Brits are racing to get their hands on electric vehicles after a surge in oil prices triggered by the war in Iran has driven away demand for petrol and diesel cars, one of the UK’s biggest used car supermarkets has said. London-listed Motorpoint, which has seen its shares jump on the back of bumper profits, said it had seen a more than doubling of sales for EVs in recent months in a “watershed moment” for the future of the car industry. Motorpoint chief executive Mark Carpenter told City AM: “EVs are probably one of the…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. becomes India’s top gas supplier, as Iran war cuts it off from the Gulf
top_signals.summary: India's gas purchases from the U.S. have grown steadily, driven by Washington’s push to sell more American energy to the South Asian country.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: US Confirms Third Strike This Week on Indian-Crewed Tankers
top_signals.summary: A U.S. jet fired two missiles into the engine room of the tanker Jalveer off Oman on Thursday, U.S. Central Command confirmed, as authorities said the 20 crew members were safe after the third strike on Indian-crewed tankers this week.Three Indian sailors died in a U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: AI Boom Stokes Inflation With Memory Chips at ‘Insane’ Prices - Bloomberg.com
top_signals.summary: AI Boom Stokes Inflation With Memory Chips at ‘Insane’ Prices  Bloomberg.com
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Siemens Gamesa CEO: Wind Turbine Cutbacks Likely if Offshore Expansion Falters
top_signals.summary: Siemens Energy on Thursday warned of possible wind turbine capacity cuts in Europe if governments fall behind in efforts to expand offshore power, saying a lack of determination could become an "existential threat" to the industry.The comments by one of Europe's biggest industrial firms reflect grow
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: South Korea's stock market rout isn't a vote against the country, market exchange chief says
top_signals.summary: "This is a rebalancing exercise, and has nothing to do with any loss of conviction in the Korean market," Korea Exchange CEO Jeong Eun-bo said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How to Trade the SpaceX IPO in Asia's Locked-Out Markets
top_signals.summary: Investors across Asia have been largely shut out of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering, which has forced them to find creative ways to make bets on SpaceX’s $75 billion global spectacle.

top_signals.title: ‘Anarchistic neobanks’ are bitcoin’s next frontier, says Blockrise CEO
top_signals.summary: Blockrise CEO Jos Lazet sees bitcoin-native institutions evolving into alternatives to banks, in an interview at BTC Prague.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: SpaceX IPO won’t ‘break’ the bull market. But investors are worried about what comes next
top_signals.summary: As far as Wall Street is concerned, the stock market has what it takes to absorb the new supply.
